auth.scheme: Authorization: Splunk <token> header on every request; the token never appears in URLs, ToString output, or exception messages

envelope.time_format: UNIX epoch seconds with millisecond precision, e.g. 1770000000.250
envelope.event_shape: the sink nests the structured record as {"level": ..., "message": ...} inside the event key
envelope.fields_rule: fields is a flat object of index-time custom fields, only sent when the record carries custom fields, and only indexed by the /event endpoint
envelope.omit_rule: optional keys that have no value (index, fields) are omitted, never sent as null

batching.body: one compact JSON envelope per event, newline-separated, POSTed in a single request
batching.byte_limit: the sink enforces a configured MaxBatchBytes on the UTF-8 request body; an event whose single envelope exceeds the limit is rejected up front
batching.order: emit order is preserved within and across batches

429.text: HEC queue is at capacity and cannot process any more requests

responses.retry_rule: retry 429/500/503 with the injected delay up to MaxAttempts total attempts, resending the identical body; other 4xx fail immediately

lifecycle.flush: Flush() delivers the pending partial batch synchronously; flushing an empty buffer sends nothing
lifecycle.dispose: Dispose() flushes pending events once; later Emit calls throw ObjectDisposedException; double dispose is a no-op; no timers or background threads decide flushing
